title: Container restart issue post rebooting the server
body:
/kind bug

I have created services from: https://github.com/docker/awesome-compose/tree/master/nginx-flask-mongo. 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/77317274/175343978-4a0068db-7516-49c0-b39d-f8f600fe409e.png)
I have changed the yml file a bit so that all the container start automatically on their own after rebooting the VM.  Added 
`restart: always` policy to reflect it.

compose.yml file: 
``` 
version: "3.3"
services:
  web:
    image: nginx
    volumes:
      - ./nginx/nginx.conf:/tmp/nginx.conf
    environment:
      - FLASK_SERVER_ADDR=backend:9091
    command: /bin/bash -c "envsubst < /tmp/nginx.conf > /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf && nginx -g 'daemon off;'"
    restart: always
    ports:
      - 80:80
    depends_on:
      - backend
  backend:
    build: flask
    restart: always
    environment:
      - FLASK_SERVER_PORT=9091
    volumes:
      - ./flask:/src
    depends_on:
      -  mongo
  mongo:
    image: mongo
    restart: always
```

All  the containers are running smoothly and I was able to curl the nginx service.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/77317274/175344595-cd6cd103-5d70-43e2-96e2-d4871dee41a8.png)

For restart policy, podman is already providing `podman-restart` systemd file which will automatically start all the exited containers which have restart policy `restart: always`. I have enable that systemd file and it is successfully loaded. 
podman-restart.service:
```
[Unit]
Description=Podman Start All Containers With Restart Policy Set To Always
Documentation=man:podman-start(1)
StartLimitIntervalSec=0

[Service]
Type=oneshot
RemainAfterExit=true
Environment=LOGGING="--log-level=debug"
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman $LOGGING start --all --filter restart-policy=always

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

After rebooting the server I am able to see all the pods up and running but they are not able to communicate with each other. I suspect it is due to networking. curl command is showing timeout error.

Podman verison: `4.0.2`
podman-compose version: `1.0.3`

I have installed podman-plugins for networking. So that, pods can communicate via CNI.

comments.body:
If you restart the pods in question, can the communicate?

I wonder if we need to wait for `network-online.target` or similar in the Podman Restart service.
yes, restarting pods manually communicate with each other. but they are not communicating after server reboot
Did you check for errors in the unit log?

podman-restart unit log file does not have any errors in it.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/77317274/175861559-a455a4f6-3e3b-453b-a6e9-b6d985e7d6e3.png)
Although I am getting below error while removing the pods
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/77317274/175861683-1345b719-7dbd-458c-8597-78ec0184d33f.png)

What distro is this? Please provide the full podman info output as request in the issue template when reporting bugs.

I assume that something like firewalld clears the iptables rules after we start the container. You either have to make sure the unit is started after firewalld or run `podman network reload --all`

Output of unit files:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/77317274/176100804-54d2fb82-1381-42f3-bf7d-ad67f1b29d35.png)

`podman network reload --all` solved the issue for me. but do we need to apply this command every time to up the containers?
Probably can be resolved with changes to `podman-restart.service` but someone more knowledgeable about systemd than I would know more.

Commit 1933972909bd should fix this issue as well as I expect the firwall issue to be addressed by
```
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
```

@jainamtrivedi-crest could you try with adding the above two lines to the `[unit]` section?

Adding mentioned lines from commit resolved the issue for now, thanks. 
I see the PR merged in main branch but have you shipped this? if yes then which version includes these changes?
It will be shipped with podman v4.2.

> Adding mentioned lines from commit resolved the issue for now, thanks.

Thank you for confirming!

> I see the PR merged in main branch but have you shipped this? if yes then which version includes these changes?

The fixes will be shipped with Podman v4.2 which will be released in mid/end July.  If you want them backported, please open a support case in the Red Hat customer channels (e.g., Bugzilla).
